title: Cineraria tenella
taxonomy: Cineraria tenella is classified under the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flower structure and leaf arrangement. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Cineraria tenella is closely related to other plants like daisies and zinnias. Within the Asteraceae family, Cineraria tenella is also classified under the genus Cineraria. The genus Cineraria is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions.

intro: Cineraria tenella, also known as the Cape marigold, is a small, annual herb native to South Africa, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and aromatic leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cineraria tenella include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cineraria tenella is often used to make topical preparations, such as creams and ointments, to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne, as well as tinctures and infusions to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation.
